title: Tangy Light Algerian Lentil Tagine
description: A fragrant, low‑calorie tagine that balances tartness from lemon and raisins with earthy lentils, perfect for a date‑night dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Mix in the tomato paste, cumin, and coriander; cook for 1 minute letting the spices toast lightly.
Pour in the rinsed lentils and water, then stir to combine.
Add the raisins, lemon zest, salt, and pepper; bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover and reduce heat to low; let the tagine steam and cook for 20–22 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the liquid is mostly absorbed.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Remove from heat, sprinkle with chopped cilantro, and serve immediately.
